clusters.title: ‘Let’s not blow it’: Trump lashes Netanyahu, urges restraint after Israel strikes Beirut
clusters.neutral_headline: ‘Let’s not blow it’: Trump lashes Netanyahu, urges restraint...
bullet_summary: Reported by The Age, ABC News Australia

items.title: ‘Let’s not blow it’: Trump lashes Netanyahu, urges restraint after Israel strikes Beirut
items.standfirst: The president has berated Israel for launching airstrikes on Lebanon that could wreck a potential deal with Iran to end months of war
items.content: The president has berated Israel for launching airstrikes on Lebanon that could wreck a potential deal with Iran to end months of war

items.title: Trump criticises Netanyahu after Israeli strikes on Beirut derail Iran peace deal
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump maintains the deal to end the conflict between the US and Iran will be signed despite Iran challenging the timeline, as he tells Israel not to carry out further attacks on Lebanon.
items.content: US President Donald Trump maintains the deal to end the conflict between the US and Iran will be signed despite Iran challenging the timeline, as he tells Israel not to carry out further attacks on Lebanon.

items.title: Anglers petition Qld government to relax bull shark fishing rules as numbers rise

items.content: The Queensland government is being petitioned to relax fishing limits for bull sharks and consider culls as anglers and waterway users fear numbers are growing.

clusters.title: Concerns new BHP agreement 'locks in' basin water extraction
clusters.neutral_headline: Concerns new BHP agreement 'locks in' basin water extraction

items.title: Concerns new BHP agreement 'locks in' basin water extraction
items.standfirst: Environmentalists and First Nations groups say a new mining agreement with BHP does not do enough to end water extraction from the Great Artesian Basin. But the SA government says the agreement imposes stricter environmental regulation.
items.content: Environmentalists and First Nations groups say a new mining agreement with BHP does not do enough to end water extraction from the Great Artesian Basin. But the SA government says the agreement imposes stricter environmental regulation.

clusters.title: 'Absolutely terrified': Scolyer's doctor reveals fears of experimental therapy
clusters.neutral_headline: 'Absolutely terrified': Scolyer's doctor reveals fears of experimental therapy

items.title: 'Absolutely terrified': Scolyer's doctor reveals fears of experimental therapy
items.standfirst: Just 11 weeks before he died, Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days. In his last major TV interview, the celebrated pathologist tells Australian Story the secret to a fulfilling life and his doctor reveals the huge risks he took to try new brain cancer treatment.
items.content: Just 11 weeks before he died, Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days. In his last major TV interview, the celebrated pathologist tells Australian Story the secret to a fulfilling life and his doctor reveals the huge risks he took to try new brain cancer treatment.

items.title: Multi-billion-dollar economic policy undermined by 'black cladding'
items.standfirst: Almost $15 billion has been spent via the federal government's Indigenous Procurement Policy, but after a decade critics say it's failed to make a significant difference to Indigenous people due to the practice of 'black cladding'.
items.content: Almost $15 billion has been spent via the federal government's Indigenous Procurement Policy, but after a decade critics say it's failed to make a significant difference to Indigenous people due to the practice of 'black cladding'.
